content.claim: A government figure publicly stated it is in 'everyone's common interest' to dispel assumptions that church-maintained schools or the 6/8-year schools would be abolished, signaling that the government perceives denominational-school stakeholders as a veto constituency requiring explicit reassurance.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: The statement is framed as preventive messaging and does not document an actual veto threat from church stakeholders
No evidence is presented of whether this reassurance has reduced or sustained veto-constituency mobilization
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
